item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say the egg would just die and be used as a sufficient DNA sample of what the (now deceased) Zygote would have developed into.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted because of really unnecessary censorship. Why would the Foundation censor which system ended up outside of the body, or what percent of the babies get defects?

Unnecessary censorship is probably my biggest pet peeve on this site, and it can ruin the immersion of an entire article for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has no effect on my vote, but I'll say this: The item does feel like a Factory item, but the note and the article title in the mainlist feel like Dr Wondertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You could use it for making D-class people

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm, although it seems like The Factory is doing something different here, it was a good article and the expungements were actually well placed in my opinion, as it left some parts to my imagination. Good work.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would congratulate you about having reached +100, but sadly I had to downvote it, let me explain why:

Bad expungement/redaction/blackboxing

a sample of blood, saliva or [REDACTED]

being born with the █████████ system positioned outside of the body

A bad attempt to copy Dr. Wondertainment products, the factory doesn't do this kind of thing

Addendum 1106-01: Transcript of label affixed to the exterior of SCP-1106

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first thing that popped into my head as I finished reading this was SCP-1080.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If the device is "intelligent" enough to know when organic and/or inorganic materials are inside, then I think it's reasonable to assume that it's "intelligent" enough to realize that its parts are its own parts, and not something foreign to the machine.
(I say "intelligent" with quotes because the machine isn't actually shown to be sentient, so it can't really be intelligent. Explaining that just in case.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea, great execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this except it doesn't feel like a Factory item. And the note read like a Wondertainment knock off. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it appears that the item will not close and lock if there is any inorganic material inside the chamber.

Since the inside of the scip is technically inorganic, wouldn't it never work?

Staff notice: this user was found to be impersonating a former staff member, and is now banned from the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's more kid-oriented.
Kids can't have kids.
Not below the age of about 14, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really fascinating thing, it's that really creepy vibe with a simple execution. When you think about it, the uses this could serve are pretty scary. Clone armies, reproduction of only the most fit humans, stuff like that. But what I wonder is what happens if you put in flawed sample— will it correct the flaw or just reproduce it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One little eyebrow-raiser: This thing is supposed to be a "more fun" way to make babies? Uh…you know…the human brain has a very powerful chemical reward system for engaging in reproductive behavior, so…I'd have to disagree with the "more fun" bit. :\ That said, giving birth isn't fun, and spending nine months as pregnant isn't fun (especially towards the end), so I guess cutting that part out too might justify saying it's equal overall? Still disagree with "more", though.

Upvoted, in any case…though I may come back to downvote when technology gets to a point where this could technically be done without violating any known scientific laws, which I think we're somewhere near right now…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I downvoted because of the blanketing and expungement. A lot of the body needs to be redone, and it would only take like five minutes to do. Otherwise, it's alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is like if the Factory was striving towards Wondertainment. Seems out-of-character for them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, anyone remember that awful movie "The Odd Life of Timothy Green"? This is what that movie SHOULD have been, and I am upvoting appropriately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simple and creepy, so great.

Here's what I feel would really add to the article without detracting anything: recovered from MC&D.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So easy to use! Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
such as a buccal scraping, or a sample of blood, saliva or [REDACTED]

If this is just listing examples, why would the report bother mentioning secret tissue? If it's supposed to be semen, I can't understand why the Foundation would redact that. Am I missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hadn't upvoted this yet apparently. Fixed that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any reason why this is listed as "Grown Your Own Child" Kit on the main list, or is that a typo?

Edit: Fixed, if that was actually intentional someone can change it back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, though I really can't think of a reason for that blank to be there. How would it be harmful or dangerous for people to know that it was the nervous vs limbic vs circulatory vs skeletal vs whatever system that was outside of the body?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. Really simple, and although we have a lot of machines that do weird stuff, it feels new.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why am I imagining that with the right input, this machine would produce a talking dog that's shaped like a yellow glove and is obsessed with rape?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really neat idea competently executed, which sometimes is all I ask for. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This feels Factory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short and sweet, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nine blanks. That narrows it down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know what it is about this article, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Dagobah system?
Sincerest apologies to everyone who read that.

Anything about the article has already been said, especially since Candlebeam already mentioned all the questions I had about it, so I'm going to leave an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if you put a fertilised human egg inside?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually thought about that while I was writing- what if there were samples from multiple people? What if you used an incomplete DNA sample? What if you used a sample from something that wasn't human? - but I was worried that going into too much detail about every possible scenario would clog up the article.

I decided to leave it up to the reader to imagine: even if you follow the correct procedure, you still can get horrendous errors, so what would happen if you didn't follow the correct procedure?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: if it's factory, it does need to have 'The Factory' engraved on it somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wondertainment tries to appeal to kids.

But this takes three months. Kids don't have that kind of attention span.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
being born with the █████████ system positioned outside of the body

I can't think of any system that wouldn't be absolutely disgusting

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nahh, Wondertainment appeals to kids, and though little girls love to play mommy, I don't think Wondertainment would do something like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems more like Dr. Wondertainment than the Factory.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice, but I wonder what would happen if you put tissue samples from multiple people inside? Would it not work, or would you get a chimera, or would one sample predominate?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't quite seem Factory to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice and simple. I like it.
